name: Pumpkin Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Use either canned pumpkin puree or make your own fresh puree:
- To cook fresh pumpkin, select a medium-sized eating pumpkin (not the jack o' lantern variety which is too stringy). Slice the pumpkin in half and scoop out the inner pulp and seeds. Place the halved pumpkin in a large roasting pan with the cut side down. The pan must have a depth of at least 1 inch to allow water to be given off as it cooks.
- Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour or until the pumpkin is tender and pierces easily with a fork. Turn the pumpkin halves over and let cool. When cooled, scrape the pulp away from the skin and puree in a blender or food mill. Pumpkin puree may be frozen for later use in a tightly covered container; it will keep about six months.
2. To make ice cream:
- Combine evaporated milk, egg yolks, brown sugar, and spices in the top of a double boiler.
- Using a wire whisk, beat the ingredients until smooth. Place the top over simmering water and cook until the custard thickens, stirring constantly.
- Remove from heat. Stir in 1 cup pumpkin puree. Set aside.
3. In a medium-sized bowl, beat egg whites, salt, and cream of tartar until stiff peaks are formed. Fold the beaten egg whites into the pumpkin custard.
4. Scrape the mixture into a freezer container or serving dish, cover tightly, and freeze until firm (about 3 hours).
5. Serve directly from the freezer. This ice cream is best if eaten within 1 week.
